summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 1, "performance": 1, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 3, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "lib/problem-solver.js", "line": "418-427", "description": "Circuit breaker cooldown does not actually stop autonomous execution. When `isCoolingDown()` is true, the code logs a warning and immediately falls back to `executeTask(parsedTask)`, so the system still runs the exact kind of task the breaker was meant to suppress. This weakens both the safety invariant and the failure-isolation intent.", "suggestion": "During cooldown, return a skipped/result-needs-human-approval outcome instead of executing. If a fallback is required, make it explicitly non-executing and surface the cooldown reason to the reporter." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "task-scheduler.js", "line": "63-64", "description": "The scheduler mixes UTC and local time in the same due-date calculation: `today` uses `toISOString()` (UTC) while `dayOfWeek` uses local `getDay()`. Around local midnight, weekly tasks can be created on the wrong date or skipped/duplicated because the stored date and weekday are computed in different timezones.", "suggestion": "Use one timezone consistently for both values. For a launchd-style local scheduler, derive both the date key and weekday from local time, or explicitly use UTC for both." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "task-scheduler.js", "line": "94-102", "description": "`lastRun` is only persisted once after the whole loop. If the process creates some issues and then crashes before `saveLastRun()`, the next run will recreate already-submitted tasks. This breaks idempotency for recurring automation.", "suggestion": "Persist `lastRun[task.id]` immediately after each successful issue creation, or write atomically after each mutation. At minimum, flush after every created task." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "lib/github-client.js", "line": "54-62", "description": "Issue polling is artificially capped to 10 open issues. If the repo has a steady stream of new labeled issues, older open items can be starved indefinitely because `task-poller` only ever sees the newest limited window. This is a correctness problem first and a throughput problem second.", "suggestion": "Paginate through all matching open issues, or at least make the limit configurable and sort/process deterministically. Pair that with local state so processed items are skipped without hiding backlog." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "task-poller.js", "line": "64-65", "description": "The poller performs an extra GitHub API call per issue to fetch `authorAssociation`, creating an N+1 pattern on every poll cycle. With more issues or slower GitHub CLI/API responses, this adds avoidable latency and increases failure surface.", "suggestion": "Fetch author association in the initial list query if possible, or batch via a single `gh api` call/GraphQL query. If batching is not available, parallelize with bounded concurrency." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "lib/task-executor.js", "line": "14-18", "description": "Path expansion and config loading are duplicated across multiple files (`lib/task-executor.js`, `lib/logger.js`, several entrypoints), despite an existing shared `path-utils.js`. This increases drift risk and makes behavior changes harder to apply consistently.", "suggestion": "Centralize shared helpers such as `expandPath()` and config loading/validation into one module, then reuse them from executors, logger, scheduler, and submitter." } ], "summary": "前提として、レビュー対象は `node_modules` を除く `~/.claude/auto-task` 配下の自前 JS と解釈した。全体として `spawn` の引数配列化や一部 Zod バリデーションなど安全寄りの設計はあるが、重大なのは problem-solver の circuit breaker が実質無効化されている点。次に、scheduler の日付計算と永続化が非原子的で、週次/日次タスクの重複作成や取りこぼしが起き得る。性能面では poller が backlog を 10 件に制限しつつ issue ごとに追加 API を叩くため、未処理タスクの滞留と API コスト増を招く。セキュリティ観点ではコマンドインジェクション対策は概ね取れているが、失敗時の安全停止という不変条件は circuit breaker 周りで崩れている。修正難易度は中程度で、優先順は 1) cooldown 中は非実行化、2) scheduler の日時基準統一と逐次保存、3) issue 取得の pagination/batching、4) 共通ユーティ...
